name: Chocolate Rum Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Butter the inside of a springform pan well and cover the outside (bottom and sides) with a sheet of heavy-duty aluminum foil, shiny side out. This reflects heat away from cheesecake and prevents it from baking too fast and becoming overcooked.
3. Mix graham cracker crumbs with 2 tbsp sugar and melted butter. Press evenly on bottom and sides of pan; refrigerate until ready to use.
4. Cut chocolate into small pieces. Melt in microwave; combine with rum. Set aside.
5. Beat cream cheese with electric mixer until light and fluffy. Gradually beat in sugar, sour cream, and vanilla. Add eggs, one at a time. Mix well.
6. Place bowl over a pan of hot water and mix until smooth (do not let water touch the bottom of the bowl).
7. Pour about 1 1/4 cups of this batter into a separate bowl and set aside.
8. Whisk remaining batter with chocolate, then stir over hot water until smooth.
9. Remove springform pan from refrigerator and fill with chocolate batter. Gently pour plain batter over top and make swirls down into the chocolate batter with a fork.
10. Place on middle rack of oven and bake for 50 minutes. Cool to room temperature, remove foil and rim of pan, and refrigerate overnight.
